name: Human Cannonball
flavor: The mobility discipline: a Sandow who treats his own body as the act's headline stunt, hurling himself across the ring as a living projectile and slamming into whatever, or whoever, is waiting on the other side.

features.name: Kinetic Buildup
features.description: You build charge the way a cannon builds pressure, through speed and impact. In addition to your baseline Grit generation, you gain 1 additional Grit the first time each turn you move at least half your speed in a straight line and then hit a creature with a melee attack before the end of that turn, and you gain 1 Grit the first time each turn a creature takes damage from your Cannon Launch — the impact of a landing slam feeding your next charge. Two separate sources, each capped at once per turn, exactly like your baseline Grit generation.

features.name: Cannon Launch
features.description: As a bonus action, spend 2 Grit to launch yourself up to 30 feet in a straight line to an unoccupied space you can see. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks and never causes fall damage from the arc, however dramatic it looks. If you end that movement within 5 feet of a hostile creature, or pass through a hostile creature's space en route (stopping in the nearest available space when you do), that creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your {{save DC|8 + proficiency bonus + Strength modifier}} or take [[2d6]] bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. You can use Cannon Launch only once per turn. Additionally, when you use Heave's fastball special to throw a willing ally, the throw's range increases to 20 feet per point of Grit spent, instead of the normal 10.

features.name: Death-Defying Stunt
features.description: Launching yourself at things is not just a combat trick — it's the act. Your long jump distance is doubled and your high jump increases by 10 feet. You land any deliberate jump or drop of 30 feet or less on your feet, unharmed and looking spectacular. You also have advantage on Charisma (Performance) and Dexterity (Acrobatics) checks made as part of a stunt.
features.sheetSummary: Long jump doubled; high jump +10 ft. Land any deliberate jump/drop of 30 ft or less unharmed. Advantage on Performance and Acrobatics checks made as part of a stunt.
